title: Tetrastigma mutabile
taxonomy: Tetrastigma mutabile is classified within the family Vitaceae, which includes other types of grapevines and related species. It belongs to the genus Tetrastigma, a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The plant is also known by its synonym, Vitis mutabilis, which indicates its historical classification under the genus Vitis. However, it is now widely accepted as a distinct species within the genus Tetrastigma. Further classification of Tetrastigma mutabile includes its order Rhamnales and class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Tetrastigma mutabile, commonly known as the "Chinese Virgin's Bower" or "Climbing Fig", is a type of flowering plant in the grape family (Vitaceae) native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrastigma mutabile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
